_doc: Maps Job Club's 9 job categories to the 88-day specified-work industry buckets used by Home Affairs. Used by eligibility.py to pick which postcode list to consult. 'null' means the category is not a recognised specified-work industry. 'ambiguous' means the category could fall under multiple industries depending on context — eligibility.py treats this as 'cannot determine without more info' and surfaces a note rather than auto-flagging.

_notes.hospitality_cleaning_caveat: Tourism/hospitality only counts as specified work in 'Northern Australia' postcodes (north of the Tropic of Capricorn). eligibility.py must check the postcode against postcodes_tourism.json — being category=hospitality is necessary but NOT sufficient.
_notes.transport_ambiguous: A driver job could be agriculture (cattle station truck), mining (mine truck), or non-qualifying (suburban delivery). Cannot decide from category alone.
_notes.events_excluded: Festival/event work is not on the Home Affairs list as of 2025-04 amendment. Always non-eligible regardless of postcode.
_notes.fishing_pearling_treefelling: These are agriculture sub-industries on the Home Affairs page; we collapse them into the 'agriculture' bucket because they share the postcode list.
